name: HTS Classification Basics
summary: Plain-language walkthrough of how to read the Harmonized Tariff Schedule and find the right 10-digit code. Start here.

name: USMCA Rule-of-Origin Guide
summary: How to tell if your goods qualify under USMCA. Covers tariff shift, regional value content, and the paperwork importers ask about most.

name: ISF Filing Checklist
summary: The 10+2 data elements, who supplies each one, and the deadline that trips people up. One page, print it.

name: Duty Drawback Primer
summary: If you import and then export, you may be leaving refund money on the table. What drawback is and when it is worth filing.

name: Customs Broker Exam Prep
summary: What the exam covers, how to study the regulations without losing your mind, and the schedule for the next sitting.

name: Trade Compliance Glossary
summary: Short definitions for the terms that come up every week: entry, bond, liquidation, HTS, ISF, FTA, and the rest.

name: Customs Bonds Explained
summary: Single-entry versus continuous bonds, how the amount is set, and why your surety asks the questions it does.

name: Customs Entry Walkthrough
summary: What happens between the ship leaving port and the goods clearing. Each step, in order, with the documents named.

name: Trade Compliance Audit Readiness
summary: What CBP looks for, the records you need to keep, and how to find your own gaps before someone else does.

name: Great Lakes Shipping Notes
summary: Why the Great Lakes ports matter to Ohio importers, seasonal closures, and how that shows up in your timelines.

name: Fall Info Night: Is a Trade Career For You?
summary: Open evening for anyone curious about customs and trade compliance work. No cost, no pressure. Bring questions.

name: Cohort 5 Kickoff Session
summary: First meeting for the new cohort. We set expectations, pair mentees with mentors, and map the next twelve weeks.

name: Hands-On HTS Classification Workshop
summary: Bring a real product. We classify it together and talk through the calls that are not obvious.

name: USMCA Origin Workshop
summary: Work through origin determinations on real bills of materials. Ryan covers the logistics side.

name: Resume and Interview Clinic
summary: Bring your resume. We mark it up and run a practice interview for broker and compliance roles.

name: Broker Exam Study Group
summary: Weekly group working through past exams together. Open to anyone sitting the next exam.

name: Cohort 6 Applications Open
summary: Applications for the winter cohort open in the fall. Spots are limited and unpaid; we keep groups small on purpose.

name: Year-End Mentee Showcase
summary: Cohort members present what they worked on. Local employers attend. Light food, real conversations.

name: How much does the program cost?
summary: Nothing. The mentorship is unpaid on both sides. We run it because the trade needs more good people.

name: Do I need a trade background to apply?
summary: No. Most of our mentees start with little or no trade experience. Curiosity and follow-through matter more.

name: How much time does it take each week?
summary: Plan on three to five hours a week over twelve weeks: one group session, some reading, and time with your mentor.

name: Is it in person or remote?
summary: Both. Group sessions are in Columbus. Mentor check-ins can be over video if that is easier for you.

name: Will the program get me a job?
summary: We do not promise jobs. We do give you the skills, the contacts, and honest feedback. The work is still yours to do.

name: Step 1: Read the program overview
summary: Start with the overview and the FAQ so you know what you are signing up for. Twelve weeks, unpaid, real work.

name: Step 2: Submit the short application
summary: Tell us who you are and why trade work interests you. A few short answers, not an essay.

name: Step 3: Have a quick call with a mentor
summary: Jason or Ryan calls you for fifteen minutes. We want to hear how you think, not quiz you on tariffs.

name: Step 4: Start with your cohort
summary: If it is a fit, you join the next cohort at kickoff. Small group, paired mentor, clear plan.
